verdict_reasoning: BAX is a cytosolic pro-apoptotic BCL2 family member that accumulates on the mitochondrial outer membrane during apoptosis to mediate membrane permeabilization and cytochrome c release. Its surface biology is restricted to the mitochondrial outer membrane, not the plasma membrane. There is no documented mechanism for BAX to reach the plasma membrane's outer leaflet under baseline, stress, or cell-death conditions. The protein remains mitochondrial-internal or mitochondrial-membrane-associated throughout its lifecycle.
